name: Loyal Bushi
blurb: Professional samurai soldier in service to a clan, met alone or in units on the march.

other: a handful of koku and bu

abilities.name: Crescent Moon Style
abilities.text:
When performing a Guard action, the bushi may spend Opportunity in the following way:
Opportunity: After another character performs an Attack action targeting the bushi or another character the bushi is guarding, the bushi may perform a Strike action targeting them. This effect persists until the start of the bushi's next turn or until they perform a Strike action.

abilities.name: Sworn Protector
abilities.text: Once per scene, when an Attack action check targeting another character at range 0–1 succeeds, the bushi may intervene, becoming the target of the action instead.

name: Humble Peasant
blurb: Hardworking commoner beneath samurai notice; covers heimin and hinin alike (hinin have status 0).

abilities.name: Overlooked
abilities.text: When a peasant makes a check targeting a character with a higher status rank, they treat that character's vigilance as 1 lower.

name: Seasoned Courtier
blurb: Politician and diplomat samurai who serves with words, relying on a yōjimbō for protection.

abilities.name: Whispering Winds
abilities.text:
Once per session as an action, the courtier may make a Courtesy (Air) check targeting one character in the scene. The TN is equal to 6 minus the target's glory rank (to a minimum of 1). If they succeed, the courtier learns a rumor about the target.
Opportunity Opportunity: The courtier learns one of the target's advantages or disadvantages of the target's choice.

abilities.name: Fanning the Flames
abilities.text:
When making a Social skill (Fire) check, the courtier may spend Opportunity in the following way:
Fire Opportunity: One target gains the Dazed condition.

name: Scholarly Shugenja
blurb: Priest who communes with the kami; rare, and seldom found outside shrines, temples, and libraries.

abilities.name: Path to Inner Peace (Invocation)
abilities.text: As a Support action, the shugenja may make a TN 2 Theology (Water) check targeting a character at range 0–2. If the shugenja succeeds, the target removes 3 fatigue, plus 1 per bonus success. A target can only be affected by the Path to Inner Peace invocation once per scene.

abilities.name: The Fires From Within (Invocation)
abilities.text:
As an Attack action, the shugenja may make a TN 3 Theology (Fire) check targeting up to three characters at range 1–3. If the shugenja succeeds, each target suffers 3 supernatural damage, plus 1 per bonus success.
Opportunity+: Choose 1 additional target per Opportunity spent this way.

abilities.name: Disciple of Secret Lore
abilities.text: Activation: Choose 0–5 additional invocations and 0–3 additional rituals that this shugenja can perform. Add 1 to this character's combat rank for each invocation with a prerequisite of rank 3+ chosen this way.

name: Trained Ashigaru
blurb: Drafted commoner foot soldier of Rokugani armies; encountered in troops when a clan is at war.

equipped: dice and cup or musical instrument
a handful of bu and zeni

abilities.name: Rank Tactics
abilities.text: When an ashigaru provides assistance to the Martial skill check of another character at range 0–2, that character adds one kept ring die set to a success result instead of rolling an additional die.

name: Venerable Provincial Daimyō
blurb: Stern, seasoned lord; rarely takes up arms anymore but remains deadly at court and in combat.

abilities.name: Lord's Command
abilities.text: Once per scene, as a Support action, a daimyō may advise a character who can hear them on how to complete a task they wish the character to perform. The character may use the daimyō's ring or skill in place of their own for the next check they make to complete this task before the end of the game session.

name: Desperate Bandit
blurb: Rural outlaw relying on swift, violent ambush of travelers on the Imperial roads.
_comment: Status is printed as '01' in the book.

abilities.name: Ambush Tactics
abilities.text: When performing an Attack action against a target with the Dazed or Disoriented condition, increase any damage dealt by 2.

name: Experienced Bandit
blurb: Cunning survivor who leads a gang of lesser outlaws or partners with a lethal rōnin.
_comment: Status is printed as '03' in the book.

abilities.name: Dirty Tricks
abilities.text:
When performing an Attack action check, an experienced bandit may spend Opportunity in the following way:
Opportunity: One character at range 0–2 suffers the Disoriented condition. Then you may move 1 range band.

abilities.name: ...Get Them, You Fools!
abilities.text: As an action, an experienced bandit may have up to two Desperate Bandits who have not yet acted this round immediately move 1 range band and perform a Strike action with skilled assistance.

name: Skillful Rōnin
blurb: Masterless samurai who fights for money but holds, on some level, to Bushidō.

abilities.name: School of the Wolf
abilities.text: Once per round, when an Attack action check targeting the rōnin succeeds, the rōnin may suffer 3 fatigue to change the target to another character (other than the attacker) at range 0–1 of the rōnin.

abilities.name: Striking as Fire
abilities.text:
When performing an Attack action (Fire) check, a rōnin may spend Opportunity as follows:
Fire Opportunity+: Increase the severity of any critical strike dealt as part of this attack by 2 per Opportunity spent this way.

name: Deadly Bloodspeaker
blurb: Effectively immortal mahō sorcerer of Iuchiban's cult, heart hidden away in a box.
_comment: Honor/status are printed as '05' and '00' in the book.

abilities.name: Bleed the Soul
abilities.text: After the Bloodspeaker inflicts a Critical Strike on a living target, the target suffers the Dazed condition and the Bloodspeaker removes 3 fatigue.

abilities.name: Hidden Heart
abilities.text: The Bloodspeaker cannot truly be vanquished until its heart (usually buried in some deadly tomb or sanctum of evil) has been destroyed. It ignores the Unconscious condition. If slain, it can possess a fresh corpse after its allies have prepared the corpse through a dark rite that takes four days to complete.

abilities.name: Master of Vile Lore
abilities.text: The Bloodspeaker is a Tainted being who can perform all mahō.

name: Wicked Mahō-Tsukai
blurb: Mortal corrupted by blood magic, serving selfish ends or Fu Leng's return.
_comment: Honor/status are printed as '01' and '00' in the book.

abilities.name: Mark of Desecration
abilities.text: As a Support action, the mahō-tsukai may make a TN 2 Theology (Earth) check targeting up to three corpses at range 0–3. If the mahō-tsukai succeeds, each target rises as a Zombie Peasant, or a Bushi Skeleton if it was a warrior in life. Then, an area encompassing 1 range band around each target's position gains the Defiled terrain quality.

abilities.name: Seeker of Vile Lore
abilities.text: The mahō-tsukai is a Tainted being. Optionally, choose 0–2 additional mahō that the mahō-tsukai can perform. Add 1 to this character's combat rank for each mahō chosen this way.

name: Bushi Skeleton
blurb: Tattered flesh, bleached bones, and undying hatred; a terrifying skeletal warrior.

abilities.name: Clattering Bones
abilities.text: A skeletal bushi is an Otherworldly, Tainted being. Additionally, the first time it would be defeated each scene, it receives a Scar disadvantage of the GM's choice instead as one of its limbs snaps or flies off.

abilities.name: Does Not Bleed
abilities.text: A skeletal bushi ignores conditions and critical strikes from non-Sacred sources.

name: Bog Hag
blurb: Regenerating Shadowlands infiltrator that wears the flayed skin of its victims as a disguise.
_comment: Honor/glory/status are printed as '00'/'05'/'00' in the book.

abilities.name: Accursed Regeneration
abilities.text: At the end of each round, if the bog hag has not suffered damage from a Sacred source this round, the bog hag removes the Lightly Injured condition or reduces the Severely Injured condition to Lightly Injured.

abilities.name: Seeker of Vile Lore
abilities.text: The bog hag is an Otherworldly, Tainted being. Optionally, choose 0–3 mahō techniques that the bog hag can perform. Add 1 to this character's combat rank for each mahō chosen this way.

abilities.name: Skins of the Slaughtered
abilities.text: As an action, a bog hag may assume the illusory form of anyone it has killed. A character may make a TN 3 Sentiment check (Earth 4, Fire 1), or another appropriate check to see through this illusion. At the GM's discretion, a character may spend Opportunity Opportunity from any check to interact with the bog hag to see one of the following: a misplaced fold in the skin, luminescence in one eye, an impossible movement of the jaw, oddly sharp teeth, the smell of viscera, a conversational fixation on fresh and bloody meat.

name: Shadowlands Goblin
blurb: Lithe, bestial horde creature; of little consequence alone, a major threat in numbers.

abilities.name: Shadowlands Creature
abilities.text: A Shadowlands goblin is a Tainted being of silhouette 1.

abilities.name: Unsightly Rabble
abilities.text:
When making an Attack action check with assistance, a goblin may spend Opportunity as follows:
Opportunity+: One character other than the target at range 0–2 suffers 4 physical damage. This Opportunity can be activated a number of times per round equal to the number of goblins assisting with the check.

name: Sinister Oni
blurb: Demonic horror of Jigoku with twisted horns, razor teeth, and enormous talons.
_comment: Honor/glory/status are printed as '00'/'04'/'00' in the book.

equipped: Demonic hide and armor scraps (Physical 5, Spiritual 5, Unholy)

abilities.name: Dread Bellow
abilities.text: Once per scene as a Scheme action, the oni may roar, feeding on the primal terror it instills. Each other character at range 0–4 must resist with a TN 4 Meditation check (Earth 2, Air 5) or suffer the Afflicted condition and stumble back 1 range band from the oni. For each character who fails, the oni removes 2 fatigue.

abilities.name: Horror of Jigoku
abilities.text: An oni is an Otherworldly, Tainted being of silhouette 4. After any character performs a successful Attack action that deals 0 damage to the oni (after resistance is applied), that character suffers 3 strife.

name: Zombie Peasant
blurb: Shambling hungering dead; the reason Rokugani funerary rites involve cremation.

abilities.name: Cadaverous Bite
abilities.text: Increase the TN of checks to resist critical strikes inflicted by zombies by 2.

abilities.name: Horrifying Reanimation
abilities.text: A zombie is an Otherworldly, Tainted being. The first time it is defeated by damage from a non-Sacred source, if a zombie is in Defiled terrain, it reanimates. At the start of its next turn, it removes all fatigue and staggers to its feet with a scar disadvantage of the GM's choice.

name: Forest Troll
blurb: Rare, intelligent but unpredictable giant of the Shinomen Mori; not above eating rude humans.
_comment: Status is printed as '05' in the book.

abilities.name: Ancient and Powerful
abilities.text: A troll is an Otherworldly being of silhouette 4.

abilities.name: Sorcerous Scales
abilities.text: After a troll suffers damage from a physical or supernatural source, increase its resistance to that damage type by 4. This effect persists until it suffers damage from a source of a different type.

name: Ki-Rin
blurb: Benevolent chimerical messenger of the kami that floats above the ground rather than trample a blade of grass.

abilities.name: Mystical Creature
abilities.text: A ki-rin is an Otherworldly being of silhouette 3 that can fly. It can move vertically and ignores the negative effects of terrain. Additionally, once per scene, it may perform an Earth or Water invocation technique, and counts as succeeding with three bonus successes and Opportunity Opportunity.

abilities.name: Sacred Envoy
abilities.text: The ki-rin can speak any language, and it knows many secrets of the world. The righteous can sense its hallowed nature; to perform an Attack action against it, a character must forfeit honor equal to their honor rank (and glory equal to their glory rank, if they are seen doing it).

abilities.name: Wrath for the Wicked
abilities.text: Once per scene as an Attack action, the ki-rin may breathe holy fire from its mouth. Each Tainted being at range 0–4 must resist with a TN 4 Fitness check (Air 3, Earth 5) or suffer 8 supernatural damage with the Sacred quality, plus additional damage equal to its shortfall, and suffer the Burning condition.

name: Manifest Air Kami
blurb: Air spirit acting directly on the world: a terrifying many-armed whirlwind of flickering hands.

abilities.name: Flight
abilities.text: A manifest air kami can move vertically and ignores the negative effects of terrain.

abilities.name: Living Air Spirit
abilities.text: A manifest air kami is an Otherworldly being of silhouette 2, 3, or 4, and may change its size as an action. Additionally, once per scene as an action of the appropriate types, a manifest air kami may perform any one Air invocation other than Rise, Air. It counts as succeeding with 3 bonus successes and 2 Opportunity.

abilities.name: Swoop
abilities.text:
When performing an Attack action check, a manifest air kami may spend Opportunity as follows:
Opportunity+: Move 1 range band away from the target per Opportunity spent this way. If the target is of silhouette 5 or smaller, the air kami may carry it to its destination instead.

name: Manifest Earth Kami
blurb: Stone-bodied earth spirit; slow to anger, but its wrath is an unstoppable avalanche.

abilities.name: Burrow
abilities.text: As a Movement action, a manifest earth kami may withdraw into the ground. It cannot be targeted by actions or effects, or perform actions other than Eruption.

abilities.name: Eruption
abilities.text: As an Attack and Movement action while burrowed, a manifest earth kami may burst from the ground at a chosen point at range 0–3. Each character at range 0–2 of that point must resist with a TN 4 Fitness check (Air 2, Water 5) or suffer 6 physical damage and the Prone condition.

abilities.name: Living Earth Spirit
abilities.text: A manifest earth kami is an Otherworldly being of silhouette 3. Additionally, once per scene as an action of the appropriate types, a manifest earth kami may perform any one Earth invocation other than Rise, Earth. It counts as succeeding with 3 bonus successes and 2 Opportunity.

name: Manifest Fire Kami
blurb: Energy given humanoid form, robed in smoke; single-minded harbinger of destruction and renewal.

abilities.name: Living Fire Spirit
abilities.text: A manifest kami is an Otherworldly being. Additionally, once per scene as an action of the appropriate types, a manifest fire kami may perform any one Fire invocation other than Rise, Fire. It counts as succeeding with 3 bonus successes and 2 Opportunity.

abilities.name: Mantle of Smoke and Ash
abilities.text: Other characters treat the area at range 0–1 of the manifest fire kami as Dangerous and Obscuring terrain.

abilities.name: Searing Caress
abilities.text:
When performing an Attack action check, a manifest fire kami may spend Opportunity as follows:
Opportunity+: The target and up to one other character per Opportunity spent this way at range 0–1 of the target suffer the Burning condition.

name: Manifest Water Kami
blurb: Shifting water spirit that can split itself to act in multiple places and reabsorb damaged copies.

abilities.name: Living Water Spirit
abilities.text: A manifest kami is an Otherworldly being. Additionally, once per scene as an action of the appropriate types, a manifest water kami may perform any one Water invocation other than Rise, Water. It counts as succeeding with 3 bonus successes and 2 Opportunity.

abilities.name: Split
abilities.text: Once per round, after a manifest water kami suffers a critical strike, it may split in two. The two new manifest water kami each have endurance equal to half that of the original, and any fatigue it had suffered is divided as evenly as possible. Then, both may remove any conditions the original was suffering. All of them act during the original water kami's turn, in the order of the GM's choosing.

name: Bear
blurb: Solitary, fiercely territorial; from large brown bears of the Shinomen to smaller black bears.

abilities.name: Crushing Swipe
abilities.text: A bear is a silhouette 3 creature with terrifying strength. When a bear inflicts a critical strike, the TN of the check to resist is 4, and if the target fails, they suffer the Dazed condition in addition to the other effects.

name: Boar
blurb: Aggressive, territorial wild pig; its jagged tusks can slay even an armored bushi.

abilities.name: Sustaining Fury
abilities.text: After a boar suffers a critical strike, it becomes Enraged. While Enraged, a boar cannot suffer the Unconscious condition and may perform Attack actions even while Incapacitated.

name: Cat
blurb: Small, cunning feline; provides entertainment, pest control, and occasional affection.

abilities.name: Pouncing Predator
abilities.text:
A cat is a silhouette 0 creature. When performing an Attack action check against an unaware or Prone target, it may spend Opportunity as follows:
Opportunity: The target suffers the Disoriented condition.

name: Dog
blurb: Devoted companion serving as guard, tracker, hunter, and fighter alongside its master.

abilities.name: Working Companion
abilities.text: Choose one Martial or Trade skill. When the dog provides assistance on a check with this skill, it counts as providing skilled assistance.

name: Hawk
blurb: Majestic lord of the skies; some samurai tame them for sport hunting.

abilities.name: Nimble Flyer
abilities.text: A hawk is a silhouette 0 creature that can fly. It can move vertically and ignores the negative effects of terrain.

abilities.name: Swoop
abilities.text:
When performing an Attack action check, a hawk may spend Opportunity as follows:
Opportunity+: Move 1 range band away from the target per Opportunity spent this way. If the target is of silhouette 0 or smaller, the hawk may carry it to its destination instead.

name: Horse (Rokugani Pony)
blurb: Native Rokugani steed; not as fierce as Unicorn-bred horses, but a steady mount.

abilities.name: Loyal Steed
abilities.text: A Rokugani pony is a silhouette 3 creature. It can be ridden by a human-sized (silhouette 2 or lower) rider.

name: Hunting Cat
blurb: Cougar, lion, or tiger; stalks and ambushes prey, mauling quickly and savagely.

abilities.name: Pouncing Predator
abilities.text:
A hunting cat is a silhouette 3 creature. When performing an Attack action check against an unaware or Prone target, it may spend Opportunity as follows:
Opportunity: The target suffers the Disoriented condition.

abilities.name: Savage Mauling
abilities.text: Disoriented targets cannot defend against damage dealt by a hunting cat.

name: Utaku Steed
blurb: Sacred steed of the Utaku Battle Maidens; fiery, ferocious, and utterly loyal.

abilities.name: Legendary Steed
abilities.text: An Utaku steed is a silhouette 3 creature. It can be ridden by a human-sized (silhouette 2 or lower) rider, but generally does not tolerate any rider other than a worthy Utaku Battle Maiden.

abilities.name: Paragon of Horses
abilities.text: A Utaku steed grants its rider skilled assistance on Martial checks they make while mounted.

name: Wolf
blurb: Pack hunter of the forested Empire; relentless pursuit and hit-and-run attacks.

abilities.name: Circle the Kill
abilities.text:
When performing a Martial Arts [Unarmed] check, a wolf may spend Opportunity in the following ways.
Water Opportunity: The target suffers the Bleeding condition.
Fire Opportunity: The target treats all terrain as Dangerous until the end of their next turn.
